title: Create Madness
description: Every (almost) Create Fabric mod used here (multiplayer support)
body:
Complex modpack with **230+** mods and multiplayer targeting

Modpack has been tested by 2 peoples in multiplayer with several days of playing and it works pretty stable. Yes, in that modpack bugs still exists, but they mostly visual. All modes, that broke gameplay has been deleted or disabled

Server files included in modpack itself, launch via `.bat` and `.sh`. Tested on a custom home server (Debian 12, java 17) and it uses `150-200`% CPU cores (Ryzen 3 3200G) and approx. `6GB + 1GB` every 12 hours. Better way to use it - reload server every night. Read `README.md` to learn more

What this modpack contains
- All core Create addons: `Crafts and Additions, New Age, Nuclear, Dreams and Desires, Food, Enchantment Industry`...
- Also added `Valkyrien Skies` and other related Create mods: `Interactive, Eureka, Clockwork, Trackwork`
- Many small mods about everything, they mostly add new recipes and adds some interesting mechanices
- `Ad Astra`...
- Added several dungeons mods: `Dungeons Arise, Create Structures, Dungeons and Taverns`
- Add many optimization mods from Modrinth. Mostly it's `Sodium, Indium and Iris Shaders`
- Using `Biomes'O Plenty` to update world generation. More mods broke server performance
- Added many UI and inventory mods, for convenience and productivity
- Added several mods from Serilum
- Mods, that without category, but you need to know about it: `PatPat, Sit, Wraith Waystones, You're in Grave Danger, Backpacks, THE COOLEST SAUSAGE`

This modpack is not about hard gameplay, it's about exploring mods and content in it. Story: we discover ultra efficient electricity engine with `3.3 kWt` output (approx. `400k` rotation force) just using different mods (`New Age + Crafts & Additions`)

My recommendations
- I didn't test in singleplayer, but I'm pretty sure if multiplayer with it ok, then singleplayer ok too
- Use Linux server, it's more performance
- Text me about your experience (@vertoker), it would be interesting to read
